title: dactylorhiza praetermissa subsp. integrata
intro: Dactylorhiza praetermissa subsp. integrata, also known as the narrow-leaved marsh orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza praetermissa subsp. integrata are said to include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as having a potential antifungal effect. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the dried roots in hot water to create a tea or by using the dried roots in a tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
